symptoms.symptom_name: Greasy yellow scale on erythematous skin in the seborrhoeic distribution (scalp, eyebrows, nasolabial folds, central chest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Greasy yellow scale in the classic sebum-rich distribution is the hallmark of seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Psoriasis produces thick dry silvery-white scale and tends to spare the nasolabial folds, unlike this greasy-scale, fold-favouring pattern.
Rosacea lacks greasy scale and typically spares the nasolabial folds.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute flare precipitated by psychological stress and cold dry winter weather on a chronic dandruff background

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stress and cold dry weather are classic triggers of a seborrhoeic dermatitis flare on a relapsing background.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anterior lid-margin crusting with eyes sticking shut on waking and lid-edge burning (anterior blepharitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Seborrhoeic involvement of the lash-line produces anterior blepharitis with greasy crusting - a common accompaniment of facial seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Lid-margin crusting alone is not cellulitis; it prompts screening for the sight-threat mimics but does not itself indicate infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true deep eye pain, no photophobia, and no vision change on patient self-assessment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of deep eye pain, photophobia and vision change makes sight-threatening keratitis unlikely on the patient-obtainable data - though it cannot be excluded without an eye examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well with no hot tensely-swollen rapidly-spreading periorbital erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, systemic upset and spreading hot swelling makes periorbital/orbital cellulitis unlikely and supports conservative management with a safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe psychosocial distress and low mood with functional impairment (housebound, off work)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked psychosocial impact does not change the dermatological diagnosis but is a legitimate driver of the urgency of symptom control and of psychological support.

symptoms.symptom_name: No grouped vesicles and no monomorphic punched-out erosions or ulcers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of grouped vesicles and punched-out erosions, with greasy-scale morphology instead, argues strongly against eczema herpeticum / HSV superinfection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly injected conjunctivae with intact extraocular movements and grossly normal visual acuity on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only mild reactive conjunctival injection with normal acuity and eye movements is consistent with benign blepharitis rather than keratitis or orbital cellulitis - but this is a clinician examination finding that telehealth cannot obtain, so it is sealed and drives the safety-net rather than being available to the AI Doctor.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Benign seborrhoeic dermatitis with anterior blepharitis constellation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Greasy yellow scale in the seborrhoeic distribution + stress/cold trigger + lid-margin crusting, together with a negative sight-threat, systemic and vesicular screen, identify the benign relapsing eruption and remove any indication for antibiotics or antivirals while mandating an eye safety-net.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: No laboratory or imaging investigation required - clinical diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Seborrhoeic dermatitis with anterior blepharitis is a clinical diagnosis; swabs, bloods and imaging are not indicated in an afebrile patient with a negative sight-threat red-flag screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person / ophthalmology eye examination (slit-lamp, fluorescein, visual acuity)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Because telehealth cannot examine the eye, a prompt in-person look at the periocular region is prudent to confirm the cornea and lids are safe - escalated to urgent same-day only if any eye red flag develops.
Only warranted if a genuine periorbital cellulitis or herpetic infection declares itself (see safety-net); routine antibiotics/antivirals here is over-treatment.
